title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs dead lettering, poison messages, delivery limits, and parking from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Dead Letter Trigger Route · changed case · Review Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 3192 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
